sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of passion fruit from the species golden passion fruit / sweet granadilla (Passiflora ligularis Juss), purple passion fruit (Passiflora edulis Sims forma edulis), yellow passion fruit (Passiflora edulis Sims forma flavicarpa) and their hybrids grown from the Passifloraceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer after preparation and packaging. Passion fruits for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties of passion fruit from the species golden passion fruit / sweet granadilla (Passiflora ligularis Juss), purple passion fruit (Passiflora edulis Sims forma edulis), yellow passion fruit (Passiflora edulis Sims forma flavicarpa) and their hybrids grown from the Passifloraceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer after preparation and packaging. Passion fruits for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of passion fruit from the species golden passion fruit / sweet granadilla (Passiflora ligularis Juss), purple passion fruit (Passiflora edulis Sims forma edulis), yellow passion fruit (Passiflora edulis Sims forma flavicarpa) and their hybrids grown from the Passifloraceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer after preparation and packaging. Passion fruits for industrial processing are excluded.

number=2

sections.title: Minimum requirements
Classification
sections.text: Passion fruits are classified into three classes defined below:
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Classification
number=2.2
level=2
Passion fruits are classified into three classes defined below:

page_spans.text: Passion fruits are classified into three classes defined below:

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Passion fruits in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free e of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
sections.retrieval_text:

number=2.2.1
level=3
Passion fruits in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free e of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Passion fruits in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free e of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Classification > Class I
number=2.2.2
level=3
Passion fruits in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:
- a slight defect in shape;
- slight defects of the skin such as scratches, not exceeding more than 10% of the total surface area of the fruit;
- slight defects in colouring.
The defects must not, in any case, affect the flesh of the fruit.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text: Passion fruits may be sized by diameter, count or weight; or in accordance with pre-existing trading practices. When such is the case, the package must be labelled accordingly.

sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: When sized by diameter
sections.text: Size is determined by the maximum diameter of the equatorial section of each fruit. The following table is a guide and may be used on an optional basis. Size Code Diameter range (mm) A > 78 B > 67 - 78 C > 56 - 67 D ≤ 56* * The minimum diameter for golden passion fruit is 56 mm

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: This class includes passion fruits which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the passion fruits retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation: - defects in shape including an extension in the zone of the stalk; - defects of the skin such as scratches or rough skin, not exceeding more than 20% of the total surface area of the fruit; - defects in colouring. The defects must not, in any case, affect the flesh of the fruit.

sections.title: MARKING OR LABELLING
sections.retrieval_text:

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only passion fruits of the same origin, variety, quality, colour and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION > Uniformity
number=5.1
level=2
The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only passion fruits of the same origin, variety, quality, colour and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

page_spans.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only passion fruits of the same origin, variety, quality, colour and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Passion fruits must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new 2, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Passion fruits shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 44-1995).

sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the passion fruits. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text:
The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CODEX STAN 193-1995).
The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

page_spans.text:
The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CODEX STAN 193-1995).
The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text:
It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/RCP 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice.
The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria related to Foods (CAC/GL 21-1997).

sentences.original: The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the passion fruits retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation: - defects in shape including an extension in the zone of the stalk; - defects of the skin such as scratches or rough skin, not exceeding more than 20% of the total surface area of the fruit; - defects in colouring.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR PASSION FRUIT
pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento 'STANDARD FOR PASSION FRUIT' e cabeçalho repetitivo 'CODEX STAN 316-2014'. Não há seções ou texto interno na página para estruturar.
Página apresenta hierarquia clara com títulos numerados de 1 a 2.2.2. Texto está associado corretamente a cada seção conforme a estrutura. A seção 2.2.2 Class I indica continuação na próxima página.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 316-2014
pages.summary: Página apresenta continuação da seção 2.2.2 da página anterior na subseção 2.2.3 Class II. A página também contém seções 3 e 4 com seus respectivos subtítulos e texto explicativo. Hierarquia numerada clara foi identificada e texto foi associado adequadamente. Não há indicações de continuidade para a próxima página nesta página.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 316-2014
pages.summary: Página apresenta hierarquia de tópicos numerados claros das seções 4.1.2 a 6.2.3, com texto explicativo associado. Identificadas seções e subseções até nível 3 com títulos e texto limpos e completos na página. Não há indicação de continuidade para outras páginas. Cabeçalho presente como 'CODEX STAN 316-2014  4' e título do documento 'CODEX STAN 316-2014' identificado.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 316-2014
pages.summary: Página apresenta seções e subseções numeradas e títulos em caixa alta, com texto explicativo associado e claro. Destaque para continuidade da seção 6.2.4 iniciada na página anterior. Títulos e texto internos estão adequadamente mapeados para RAG, respeitando níveis e associações corretas.
